title: Intense Garlicky Jamaican Callaloo Frittata
description: A flavorful and spicy Jamaican-inspired frittata filled with callaloo, garlic, and cheese, perfect for a flexitarian brunch
instructions: Heat the rapeseed oil in a large skillet over medium heat
Add the minced garlic and saute for 1 minute until fragrant
Add the chopped onion and diced scotch bonnet pepper and saute for 3 minutes until the onion is translucent
Add the chopped callaloo and saute for 2 minutes until wilted
In a large bowl, whisk together the beaten eggs and a pinch of salt and pepper
Pour the egg mixture over the callaloo mixture in the skillet
Sprinkle the shredded cheddar cheese over the top of the egg mixture
Transfer the skillet to the oven and bake at 375 degrees Fahrenheit for 20 minutes until the eggs are set
Remove the skillet from the oven and let it cool for 2 minutes
Use a spatula to carefully loosen the edges of the frittata from the skillet
Transfer the frittata to a plate and serve hot, garnished with chopped fresh herbs if desired
